name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of expertise in vibrational spectroscopy (FT-IR, Raman), thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), and polymer characterization. Proven track record in method development, materials analysis, and failure mode identification for industrial polymer systems. Experienced in analytical instrumentation calibration, SOP development, and client-focused reporting in fast-paced laboratory environments.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading polymer process development with extensive FT-IR and thermal characterization to establish structure-property relationships in bio-based polymer systems.
Developing analytical methods for polymer quality control, including TGA for thermal stability assessment and FT-IR for chemical composition verification.
Conducted comprehensive materials characterization using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, and DSC to analyze organic and organometallic thin-film materials.
Identified material failure mechanisms through vibrational spectroscopy and thermal analysis, developing mitigation strategies for 15+ material variants.
Operated and maintained multiple analytical instruments including FT-IR, UV-Vis spectrophotometers, TGA, and DSC systems.
Characterized molecular thin-film coatings using FT-IR and ellipsometry to optimize film morphology and optical performance for defense applications.
Developed surface analysis protocols and established quality control criteria for CVD-based coating processes.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using in-situ QCM-D monitoring and FT-IR characterization to ensure coating uniformity and reproducibility.
Established process control parameters based on analytical data, improving batch-to-batch consistency.
Managed analytical instrumentation including FT-IR, UV-Vis, TGA, and DSC systems for 15-member research group.
Implemented instrument calibration schedules and maintenance protocols, improving analytical uptime by 30%.
Developed standardized operating procedures (SOPs) for analytical methods and safety protocols.
Coordinated multi-project resource planning and cross-functional collaboration with external partners.

projects.title: Polymer Thermal Stability Assessment
projects.description: Developed comprehensive TGA/DSC protocols for evaluating thermal degradation and glass transition behavior in bio-based polymer systems.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Characterized molecular thin-film optical coatings using FT-IR and ellipsometry to achieve performance specifications for defense applications.
projects.tech: FT-IR, ellipsometry, surface analysis, CVD processes

projects.title: Materials Failure Analysis
projects.description: Systematically identified failure modes in functional materials through vibrational spectroscopy and thermal analysis.
projects.tech: FT-IR, TGA, DSC, failure mode analysis, DOE

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
